title: Design a subcommand structure
description: Organize a multi-action tool into a verb/noun subcommand tree with consistent global and per-command flags and discoverable help.
body: Steps: 1. Enumerate the actions the tool performs and group them into subcommands (e.g. `tool create`, `tool list`). 2. Decide global flags (apply everywhere, like --verbose) vs per-subcommand flags. 3. Give each subcommand its own help and usage, plus a top-level help listing subcommands. 4. Make unknown subcommands and missing required args fail with a usage error (exit 2). 5. Keep naming and flag conventions consistent across subcommands. Rollback point: before renaming an existing command (a breaking change for scripts). Validation: `tool --help` and `tool <cmd> --help` both work and exit 0, unknown commands error clearly; inconsistent flag names across subcommands is a UX defect to fix.
